# supablock

> Browse and verify a Supabase account as a read-only filesystem. supablock
> mirrors the Supabase Management API (organizations, projects, config, API
> keys, edge functions, storage buckets, auth providers, branches) and each
> project's table rows as a directory tree. Every request is a GET — the
> tool physically cannot create, change or delete anything.

Two ways to read the tree:

- `supablock ls [path]` / `supablock cat <path>` — resolve tree paths
  straight off the API. Works anywhere: no FUSE, no privileges, no mount,
  no daemon. This is the mode for AI agents, CI, and restricted sandboxes.
- `supablock mount ~/Supabase` — a real FUSE mount (kernel-enforced
  read-only) for `grep -r`, `find` and `diff` across the whole account
  with a shared cache.

Authentication for non-interactive use: set `SUPABLOCK_TOKEN=sbp_…` (a
personal access token from supabase.com/dashboard/account/tokens). It
overrides any stored credential and is never written to disk. Interactive
use: `supablock login`.

Quick start for an agent with shell access:

    curl -fsSL https://filipecabaco.github.io/supablock/install.sh | sh
    export SUPABLOCK_TOKEN=sbp_...          # ask the user for this
    supablock ls organizations
    supablock ls organizations/<org>/projects
    supablock cat organizations/<org>/projects/<ref>/health
    supablock cat organizations/<org>/projects/<ref>/config/auth.json

Or via Docker, with nothing installed (image: filipecabaco/supablock):

    docker run --rm -e SUPABLOCK_TOKEN=$SUPABLOCK_TOKEN \
      filipecabaco/supablock cat organizations/<org>/projects/<ref>/health

Tree shape (all paths under the root):

    organizations/<org>/{info.json,members.json,regions.json}
    organizations/<org>/projects/<ref>/info.json
    organizations/<org>/projects/<ref>/health
    organizations/<org>/projects/<ref>/config/{auth,database,realtime,storage}.json
    organizations/<org>/projects/<ref>/config/auth/sso/<id>/info.json
    organizations/<org>/projects/<ref>/config/auth/third-party/<id>/info.json
    organizations/<org>/projects/<ref>/api-keys/{publishable,secret}
    organizations/<org>/projects/<ref>/functions/<slug>/{info.json,body}
    organizations/<org>/projects/<ref>/storage/buckets/<name>/info.json
    organizations/<org>/projects/<ref>/branches/<branch>/info.json
    organizations/<org>/projects/<ref>/database/<schema>/<table>/rows-000000.csv

Facts agents should know: JSON output is deterministic (sorted keys,
pretty-printed, trailing newline) so diffs are clean; exit codes are 0 ok,
1 usage/no-such-path, 2 not authenticated, 3 API/network/rate-limited,
4 environment; the Management API allows 120 requests/minute (batch reads:
`supablock cat pathA pathB` amortizes one process and one cache);
`api-keys/secret` stays REDACTED unless the user opts in.

## Links

- [Full documentation](https://filipecabaco.github.io/supablock/)
- [README](https://github.com/filipecabaco/supablock)
- [Agent skill (SKILL.md)](https://github.com/filipecabaco/supablock/blob/main/skills/supablock/SKILL.md):
  install with `npx skills add filipecabaco/supablock`
- [Docker image](https://hub.docker.com/r/filipecabaco/supablock)
- [Installer](https://filipecabaco.github.io/supablock/install.sh)
